license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.yaffsbn
title: PCL:PEG Electrospinning
author: Kenneth Schackart

steps_raw.instruction: In 50 mL tube, add 14 mL chloroform and 6 mL acetone. This is your solvent solution. The solvent solution should be 70% chloroform and 30% acetone. You can adjust volumes accordingly. Always work with these solvents in chemical fume hood.
In 15 mL tube dissolve 1 g PCL in 10 mL solvent solution to make a 10 Mass / % volume solution of PCL in solvent.
In a second 15 mL tube dissolve 500 mg PEG in 10 mL solvent solution to make a 5 Mass / % volume solution of PEG in solvent.
Place the solvent solutions on a rocker overnight to fully dissolve.
Fold a 10 cm × 10 cm piece of aluminum foil.
Attach foil square to copper plate.
Using double-sided tape, attach cover slips onto aluminum foil.
Face the copper plate normal to the syringe pump within the enclosure, and move until the plate is approximately 9 cm away from the needle. Increasing the distance will decrease fiber diameter.
Vortex the PCL:PEG solution for 3 minutes
Combine the polymer solutions at a rate of 3 parts PCL solution to 1 part PEG solution.
Pour PCL:PEG solution onto petri dish and draw solutin into a 10 mL syringe with 23 ga. tip.
Purge any bubbles from the syringe by holding the syringe with the tip facing upward and depressing the plunger.
Load syringe onto syringe pump, and secure.
Clip the ground (black) wire to the leads coming from the copper plate. Clip positive (red) wire to the syringe needle.
Set syringe pump rate to 20 µL/min, and begin pumping.
Turn on power source set to 15,000 V. Do not touch any of the power source leads. Turn off power source to make any adjustments to the setup.
If necessary, "aim" the needle/syringe pump to obtain a uniform layer on all coverslips.
If clumping occurs at the needle, use a non-conducting material to clear.
Once a desired thickness has been spun, turn off power supply and allow to cool for 2-3 min.
Detach power supply leads.
Use a blade to cut the fibers between the cover slips. Use caution not to disturb the fibers on the cover slips.
Remove cover slips carefully.
